In column 4, most of the values are a 3 letter word.
Some of the values in column 4 contain 4 letters.
I would like to add a condition to the following code that checks column 4 so that it uses the first 3 letters as the Target Value in the code below.

If Target.Column = [4] Then
Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Target.Value) 'Set To the sheet named in the clicked cell
wsd.Activate

If Target.Column = 4 Then 'Check if clicked cell is in column 4
    If Len(Target.Value) > 3 Then 'Check if value in clicked cell has more than 3 letters
        Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Left(Target.Value, 3)) 'Set To the sheet named with the first 3 letters
        wsd.Activate
    Else
        Set wsd = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(Target.Value) 'Set To the sheet named in the clicked cell
        wsd.Activate
    End If
End If
I need a VBA code that will select the first empty space in column A

Here's a VBA code that will select the first empty space in column A:

```
Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
    Dim i As Long
    
    With ActiveSheet
        i = .Cells(.Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row + 1 ' Find the last used row in column 1 and add 1 to get the next row
        .Range("A" & i).Select ' Select the first empty cell in column A
    End With
End Sub
```

This code finds the last used row in column 1 using the `End(xlUp)` method, adds 1 to get the next row, and then selects the first empty cell in column A using the `Range("A" & i).Select` method.

```

Application.ScreenUpdating = False ' Turn off screen updating

' Your code goes here

Application.EnableEvents = True ' Turn on events
Application.ScreenUpdating = True ' Turn on screen updating
```

My problem is that there is another code that  is activated by F2. The code checks if A2:E2 and if any of the cells are empty, it pumps up a Message Box.

To disable the code that is activated by F2 while your main code is running, you can use the `Application.OnKey` method to temporarily disable F2 as a hotkey. Here's how you can integrate it into your code:

```
Sub RequestToContractorXYZ()

Dim wsjrx As Worksheet
Dim wscfx As Worksheet
Set wsjrx = Worksheets("Job Request")
Set wscfx = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)

' Disable F2 hotkey
Application.OnKey "{F2}", ""

Dim lastRow As Long
lastRow = wscfx.Cells(wscfx.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1
wscfx.Range("B" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("B2").Value
wscfx.Range("C" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("F2").Value
wscfx.Range("D" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("A21").Value
wscfx.Range("F" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("C2").Value
wscfx.Range("J" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("E2").Value
wscfx.Range("L" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("D2").Value

Application.EnableEvents = False ' Turn off events
Application.ScreenUpdating = False ' Turn off screen updating

wsjrx.Range("A2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("B2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("C2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("D2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("E2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("F2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("E5:E20").ClearContents

wscfx.Select

' Re-enable F2 hotkey
Application.OnKey "{F2}", "YourF2Code"

Application.EnableEvents = True ' Turn on events
Application.ScreenUpdating = True ' Turn on screen updating

End Sub
```

Replace `"YourF2Code"` with the name of the macro that is executed by F2. This will disable the F2 hotkey while your main code is running, and re-enable it once your code is finished.

To prevent the other code that is activated by Column F2 cell from running while your `RequestToContractorXYZ` code is executing, you can also temporarily disable events for that code using the `Application.EnableEvents` property.

Here's how you can modify your code to temporarily disable the other code while your `RequestToContractorXYZ` code is running:

```
Sub RequestToContractorXYZ()

Dim wsjrx As Worksheet
Dim wscfx As Worksheet
Set wsjrx = Worksheets("Job Request")
Set wscfx = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)

' Disable F2 hotkey
Application.OnKey "{F2}", ""

' Disable event for the other code
Application.EnableEvents = False

Dim lastRow As Long
lastRow = wscfx.Cells(wscfx.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1
wscfx.Range("B" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("B2").Value
wscfx.Range("C" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("F2").Value
wscfx.Range("D" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("A21").Value
wscfx.Range("F" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("C2").Value
wscfx.Range("J" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("E2").Value
wscfx.Range("L" & lastRow).Value = wsjrx.Range("D2").Value

Application.EnableEvents = False ' Turn off events
Application.ScreenUpdating = False ' Turn off screen updating

wsjrx.Range("A2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("B2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("C2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("D2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("E2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("F2").ClearContents
wsjrx.Range("E5:E20").ClearContents

wscfx.Select

' Re-enable F2 hotkey
Application.OnKey "{F2}", "YourF2Code"

' Re-enable event for the other code
Application.EnableEvents = True

Application.EnableEvents = True ' Turn on events
Application.ScreenUpdating = True ' Turn on screen updating

End Sub
```

Replace `"YourF2Code"` with the name of the macro that is executed by the other code. This will disable the other code while your `RequestToContractorXYZ` code is running, and re-enable it once your code is finished.

I would like to change the following code to find the last cell in Column B that has a value and if the cell in column A of the same row is blank, the select the empty cell in column A and pop up the message:  With ActiveSheet
i = .Cells(.Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row + 1 ' Find the last used row in column 1 and add 1 to get the next row
.Range("A" & i).Select ' Select the first empty cell in column A
End With
MsgBox ("Please Enter the Name of the Person / Contractor that will be performing ADHOC activity")

End Sub

Here is the modified code that will find and select the first empty cell in Column A, as described:

```
Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim emptyCell As Range
    
    ' Find the last used row in column B
    lastRow = Cells(Rows.Count, 2).End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Check each row and look for empty cells in Column A starting from the last row
    For i = lastRow To 1 Step -1
        If Cells(i, 1).Value = "" Then
            Set emptyCell = Cells(i, 1)
            Exit For
        End If
    Next i
    
    ' If an empty cell is found, select it and show the message box
    If Not emptyCell Is Nothing Then
        emptyCell.Select
        MsgBox ("Please Enter the Name of the Person / Contractor that will be performing ADHOC activity")
    End If
End Sub
```

This modified code finds the last used row in Column B using the `End(xlUp)` method. It then checks each row starting from the last row to look for an empty cell in Column A. When it finds an empty cell, it selects it and shows the message box using the `MsgBox` function.

You can call this macro from your other macro using `Call SelectFirstEmptyCell`.
When the last used row in column B is found, I only need to check if the  cell in column A on the same row as the last used row of column B is empty

In that case, you can modify the previous code to only check the last row of Column B instead of looking for empty cells in Column A:

```
Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
    Dim lastRowB As Long
    
    ' Find the last used row in column B
    lastRowB = Cells(Rows.Count, 2).End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Check if the cell in column A on the last row of column B is empty
    If Cells(lastRowB, 1).Value = "" Then
        Cells(lastRowB, 1).Select ' Select the first empty cell in column A
        MsgBox ("Please Enter the Name of the Person / Contractor that will be performing ADHOC activity")
    End If
End Sub
```

This modified code finds the last used row in Column B using the `End(xlUp)` method and then checks if the cell in Column A on the same row is empty. If it is empty, it selects the empty cell and shows the message box using the `MsgBox` function.

Is it possible to run macro on an excel sheet1 only if the sheet1 was called by a vba code from sheet9.

If the previous active sheet was sheet8 and you move to sheet1 the macro in sheet1 will not run.

If the previous active sheet was sheet9 and you move to sheet1 the macro in sheet1 will not run.

But if a macro in sheet sheet9 opens causes sheet1 the macro in sheet1 will now run.

Yes, it is possible to run a macro on Sheet1 only if it was called by a VBA code from Sheet9. 

Here is one way to accomplish this:

1. Start by creating a Public variable at the top of your code module for Sheet9:

```
Public sheet9Active As Boolean
```

2. Next, in the code on Sheet9 that opens Sheet1, set the `sheet9Active` variable to `True` before activating Sheet1:

```
Sub OpenSheet1FromSheet9()
    sheet9Active = True ' Set sheet9Active to True
    Sheet1.Activate ' Activate Sheet1
End Sub
```

3. In the Sheet1 code module, add the following code at the top of each event handler or macro that you only want to run when Sheet1 is opened from Sheet9:

```
If Not sheet9Active Then Exit Sub
```

This code checks whether `sheet9Active` is `True`. If it is not `True`, then the code exits the sub or event handler without running the rest of the code.

4. Finally, in the code for any other macro that changes the ActiveSheet, add the following line before activating the sheet:

```
sheet9Active = False
```

This code sets `sheet9Active` to `False` before activating the new sheet, which will prevent the Sheet1 code from running if it is opened from the new sheet instead of from Sheet9.

With these changes, the macro on Sheet1 will only run when it is opened from Sheet9. If the previous active sheet was Sheet8 or any other sheet, the macro will not run. If a macro
